text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。我是Neil。我是Bekka。

text: Neil, do you believe that life exists away from planet Earth?
zh: [1] Neil,你相信地球以外存在生命吗?

text: Well, I'm not sure about the kind of little green men that you see in science fiction movies.
zh: [2] 呃,像科幻电影里那种小绿人,我不太确定。

text: But also, I think we cannot be alone in the universe.
zh: [3] 但我确实认为,宇宙中不可能只有我们。

text: What about you, Bekka? What do do you think? I agree. I think that maybe life does exist beyond our planet,
zh: [4] Bekka,你怎么看?我同意。我觉得地球以外或许真的存在生命,

text: but maybe not as we see in alien movies.
zh: [5] 但也许不是外星电影里那种样子。

text: Well, in this episode we'll be learning from science and space experts
zh: [6] 在这一集里,我们将向科学和太空专家学习,

text: about whether the conditions for life exist on a planet 40 light -years away.
zh: [7] 了解一颗40光年外的行星是否具备生命存在的条件。

text: Yes, and as usual, you can find a transcript for this episode,
zh: [8] 是的,和往常一样,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到这期节目的文字稿、

text: along with all the vocabulary and a worksheet, on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [9] 词汇表和练习题。

text: OK, a quiz question for you, Becca. How long would it take to drive a car to the sun at 100 kilometres an hour?
zh: [10] 好了,给你出个小测验,Becca。以每小时100公里的速度开车到太阳,需要多长时间?

text: Is it a. 70 years, b. 170 years, or c. 1 ,700 years?
Well, Neil, the sun seems very far away, so I'll go for c. 1 ,700 years.
zh: [12] 呃,Neil,太阳看起来非常远,我选c. 1700年。

text: I hope you have enough petrol for that. Well, we'll find out the answer to the question later.
zh: [13] 希望你有足够的汽油。好吧,答案我们稍后揭晓。

text: Now though, some science and space experts are excited about a planet in a different solar system from our own.
zh: [14] 不过现在,一些科学和太空专家正为一颗位于不同太阳系的行星感到兴奋。

text: Science journalist Caroline Steele talks about this discovery on the BBC World Service programme What in the World?
zh: [15] 科学记者Caroline Steele在BBC世界服务节目《What in the World?》中谈到了这一发现。

text: So scientists have been looking at an exoplanet,
zh: [16] 科学家们一直在研究一颗系外行星,

text: so that's a planet that exists in a solar system that isn't our own, called TRAPPIST -1e.
zh: [17] 也就是存在于我们太阳系之外的行星,名叫TRAPPIST-1e。

text: And it's a rocky planet like Earth. It's It's a similar size to Earth
zh: [18] 它是一颗像地球一样的岩石行星,大小与地球相近,

text: and it exists in the habitable zone around its sun. And its sun is called Trappist
zh: [19] 并且位于其恒星周围的宜居带内。它所环绕的恒星叫做TRAPPIST

text: -1. Caroline explains that scientists have discovered that the planet Trappist -1e exists
zh: [20] -1。Caroline解释说,科学家们发现,TRAPPIST-1e这颗行星位于

text: in the habitable zone around its sun. The adjective habitable describes a place or habitat that is suitable for something to live.
zh: [21] 其恒星周围的宜居带内。形容词habitable(宜居的)用来描述适合生物生存的地方或栖息地。

text: Yes, and a zone is an area with a particular feature. future.
zh: [22] 没错,zone(区域)指具有某种特征的地区。

text: For example, people who live in earthquake zones have to be prepared for danger.
zh: [23] 比如,生活在地震带的人们必须为危险做好准备。

text: So a habitable zone is an area where something could live.
zh: [24] 所以,宜居带就是可能有生物生存的区域。

text: Caroline goes on to explain why they think TRAPPIST -1e may be habitable.
zh: [25] Caroline接着解释了为什么他们认为TRAPPIST-1e可能宜居。

text: Scientists have been looking at the potential atmosphere around TRAPPIST -1e and they've ruled out that it's really full of hydrogen or carbon dioxide,
zh: [26] 科学家们一直在研究TRAPPIST-1e可能存在的大气层,他们已经排除了它富含氢气或二氧化碳的可能性,

text: which is a great thing because they're greenhouse gases which would make the planet really hot.
zh: [27] 这是个好消息,因为这些是温室气体,会让行星变得非常炎热。

text: And there are some small clues that that it might be a nitrogen -dominated atmosphere,
zh: [28] 而且有一些小线索表明,它的大气层可能以氮气为主,

text: which, again, is another sort of clue that there could be life there.
zh: [29] 这也是另一个可能存在生命的线索。

text: Caroline Steele says that scientists are looking at the potential atmosphere around TRAPPIST -1e.
zh: [30] Caroline Steele说,科学家们正在研究TRAPPIST-1e可能存在的大气层。

text: That is, they want to find out if the atmosphere is capable or likely to become able to host life.
zh: [31] 也就是说,他们想弄清楚这个大气层是否有能力、或者有可能发展出能够支持生命的条件。

text: If something has potential, it already has the qualities or abilities to do something.
zh: [32] 如果某物具有potential(能力),它已经具备做某事的素质或能力。

text: The adjective potential has a similar meaning but refers to future capability.
zh: [33] 形容词potential(潜在的)意思类似,但指的是未来的能力。

text: Exactly. We could say the data has potential
zh: [34] 没错。我们可以说这些数据有潜力(has potential),

text: in that it shows signs of helping the research. But when we talk about potential data, we're referring to data that could help us with future research.
zh: [35] 意思是它显示出有助于研究的迹象。但当我们说“potential data”(潜在数据)时,指的是可能有助于未来研究的数据。

text: She also mentions how scientists have ruled out that TRAPPIST -1e is full of hydrogen or carbon dioxide.
zh: [36] 她还提到科学家们已经排除了TRAPPIST-1e富含氢气或二氧化碳的可能性。

text: The phrasal verb rule out means to dismiss the possibility or idea of something.
zh: [37] 短语动词rule out的意思是排除某种可能性或想法。

text: Scientists have found that Trappist -1e is not full of those greenhouse gases. They've ruled it out.
zh: [38] 科学家们发现TRAPPIST-1e并不富含那些温室气体,他们已经排除了这种可能性。

text: Indeed. And Caroline goes on to say how the atmosphere of Trappist -1e is dominated by
zh: [39] 没错。Caroline接着说,TRAPPIST-1e的大气层以

text: nitrogen. If something is dominated by something else, it is controlled by it or is the most significant element of it.
zh: [40] 氮气为主。如果某物被另一物dominated(主导),意味着它受其控制,或者该物是其中最主要的成分。

text: Trappist -1e is full of nitrogen, which is exciting because nitrogen Nitrogen is a sign of life and habitability.
zh: [41] TRAPPIST-1e富含氮气,这令人兴奋,因为氮气是生命和宜居性的标志。

text: So what if nitrogen is actually found on this planet? Caroline Steele has more.
zh: [42] 那么,如果这颗行星上真的存在氮气,又意味着什么呢?Caroline Steele继续说道。

text: My guess is what we'll do is try and see what other gases are in the atmosphere and use models to work out how warm the planet could be
zh: [43] 我猜接下来我们会尝试看看大气中还有哪些气体,并利用模型推算这颗行星可能有多温暖,

text: and whether or not it's a temperature that basically could support life or maybe it's too hot or too cold.
zh: [44] 以及它的温度是否基本上能够支持生命,或者是不是太热或太冷。

text: But fingers crossed it has nitrogen, there's enough of it to keep the planet a decent temperature and then that's one step closer to finding life.
zh: [45] 但但愿(fingers crossed)它含有氮气,而且含量足够让这颗行星维持一个适宜的温度,那样我们就离发现生命更近了一步。

text: Caroline guesses that scientists will focus on the planet's temperature and whether it could support life.
zh: [46] Caroline猜测科学家们接下来会关注这颗行星的温度,以及它是否能够支持生命。

text: She uses the expression fingers crossed to express hope for a positive outcome.
zh: [47] 她用fingers crossed这个表达来表示希望有好结果。

text: Yes, we often make the physical gesture of crossing our fingers for good luck.
zh: [48] 是的,我们常常会用交叉手指的动作来祈求好运。

text: We do. That reminds me, Neil, the answer to your question, fingers crossed that I answered correctly.
zh: [49] 没错。这让我想起来,Neil,你那个问题的答案——但愿我答对了。

text: I see what you've done there, Becca. Yes, I asked you how long
zh: [50] 我看出你的用意了,Becca。是的,我问你

text: it would take to drive to the sun at 100 kilometres an hour and you answered c 1700 years.
zh: [51] 以每小时100公里的速度开车到太阳需要多长时间,你回答的是c. 1700年。

text: Well, the Sun is very far away, but the answer was b only 170 years.
zh: [52] 呃,太阳确实非常遥远,但正确答案是b,只需要170年。

text: Ah well, that's still longer than my lifetime. Now let's have a recap of the language
zh: [53] 啊,那也比我这辈子还长呢。现在让我们回顾一下这期节目学到的内容,

text: we've learnt in this episode, starting with habitable, which describes a place or habitat that is suitable for something to live.
zh: [54] 首先是habitable(宜居的),用来描述适合生物生存的地方或栖息地。

text: A zone is an area with a particular feature. Potential describes having possible qualities or abilities to do something.
zh: [55] zone(区域)指具有某种特征的地区。potential(潜在的)描述具备做某事的可能素质或能力。

text: The phrasal verb rule out means to dismiss the possibility or idea of something.
zh: [56] 短语动词rule out意思是排除某种可能性或想法。

text: We also had dominated. If something is dominated by something else, it is controlled by it or is the most significant element of it.
zh: [57] 我们还学了dominated(主导)。如果某物被另一物主导,意味着它受其控制,或者该物是其中最主要的成分。

text: And finally, the expression fingers crossed is used to wish for hope or luck.
zh: [58] 最后是表达fingers crossed,用来表示希望或祈求好运。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com for a quiz and worksheet for this episode.
zh: [59] 我们的六分钟又到时间了,欢迎前往我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,获取本期的小测验和练习题。

text: See you soon. Goodbye for now. Bye.
zh: [60] 我们下次再见,再会。再见。
